samples.raw_output:
```json
{
    "cefr_level": "B1",
    "grammar_score": 0.6,
    "corrections": [
        "i -> I",
        "simular -> similar",
        "Here is the tips -> Here are the tips",
        "this apps -> these apps",
        "spend a lot of time to TV -> spend a lot of time on TV",
        "leave from useless groups -> leave useless groups",
        "at VK -> on VK",
        "enought -> enough",
        "I hope, my tips -> I hope my tips",
        "write me at personal messages -> send me a private message"
    ],
    "feedback": "The student is able to communicate a clear message and provide structured advice, which is characteristic of the B1 level. While there are several grammatical errors (subject-verb agreement, preposition usage, and spelling), they do not hinder the reader's understanding. The use of a numbered list shows good organization, and the vocabulary is appropriate for the task, though simple."
}
```

```json
{
    "cefr_level": "B2",
    "grammar_score": 0.75,
    "corrections": [
        "I chose the same way of studying, I try..." -> "I chose the same way of studying; I try..." (Comma splice),
        "decition" -> "decision",
        "tips how to get it" -> "tips on how to get it",
        "options how to organize" -> "options for organizing",
        "each my day" -> "each of my days" or "my days",
        "rest within them" -> "rest during them",
        "other's works" -> "others' work" (Work is uncountable in this context),
        "But very important thing" -> "But a very important thing is"
    ],
    "feedback": "The student demonstrates a strong B2 level. They use a variety of discourse markers ('Actually', 'However', 'For example', 'Also') to create a cohesive and logical flow. The vocabulary is advanced and used naturally, with a particularly good use of the word 'abuse' in a metaphorical sense. While there are several grammatical errors (comma splices, spelling of 'decision', and issues with uncountable nouns like 'works'), these do not impede communication. The sentence structures are complex and the tone is perfectly appropriate for the task."
}
```

```json
{
    "cefr_level": "B1",
    "grammar_score": 0.5,
    "corrections": [
        "I want to tell you about two of my own tips, which help me stay happy...",
        "Firstly, I recommend communicating more with other people.",
        "They can help you look at life more simply.",
        "In the future, good networking will give you a lot of opportunities.",
        "Secondly, you need to track your time; practice time management.",
        "If you think that you study all the time and don't have free time, try noting your day.",
        "In my experience, all the guys who do this find some bad habits that take up really a lot of time.",
        "Delete them and live more freely.",
        "I believe my tips will help you.",
        "If not, you are unlucky..."
    ],
    "feedback": "The student demonstrates a B1 level of proficiency. While there are frequent spelling errors ('abot', 'reccommend', 'futcher', 'expirience', 'beleave') and grammatical slips (especially with verb patterns and word order), the student is attempting to express complex ideas and provide structured advice. The use of discourse markers ('Firstly', 'Secondly', 'Also') and advanced vocabulary like 'networking' and 'time management' pushes this above an A2 level. The meaning remains clear throughout the text despite the errors."
}
```

```json
{
    "cefr_level": "B1",
    "grammar_score": 0.6,
    "corrections": [
        "Hellow -> Hello",
        "and me to -> and me too",
        "In first semester -> In the first semester",
        "wery big problem -> a very big problem",
        "how can I fix it -> how I can fix it",
        "I will give you them -> I will share them with you",
        "some task-management system -> a task-management system",
        "enouth -> enough",
        "bust your productivity -> boost your productivity"
    ],
    "feedback": "The student demonstrates a B1 level of proficiency. While there are several spelling errors ('wery', 'enouth', 'hellow') and some grammatical slips regarding articles and word order, the student successfully uses more advanced vocabulary such as 'prioritize', 'task-management system', and 'productivity'. The text is well-organized, the meaning is clear throughout, and the student is able to provide structured advice to solve a problem, which is characteristic of an independent user."
}
```

```json
{
    "cefr_level": "B1",
    "grammar_score": 0.5,
    "corrections": [
        "It is easily -> It is easy",
        "pass all exam -> pass all exams",
        "finish the university -> graduate from university",
        "I want to some tips for became diffiecult study easilier -> I want to give some tips to make difficult studies easier",
        "rewrite solution from other students -> copy solutions from other students",
        "what your friend write -> what your friend wrote",
        "in future -> in the future",
        "It is not obviously tip -> It is not an obvious tip",
        "it really work -> it really works",
        "your brain relax and do not do anything -> your brain relaxes and clears",
        "solve spend less time -> spend less time",
        "More time of sleeping -> More sleep",
        "It is obviously -> It is obvious",
        "aproximately -> approximately",
        "for working -> working",
        "Do homework after getting -> Do homework as soon as you receive it",
        "dont spend time -> you won't spend time",
        "better mark -> better marks",
        "if you sleep in first iteration -> if you slept during the first attempt",
        "making you eductation esalier -> making your education easier"
    ],
    "feedback": "The student demonstrates a B1 level of proficiency. While there are frequent grammatical errors (especially with adjectives/adverbs like 'easily' vs 'easy' and subject-verb agreement), the student is able to express complex ideas and provide a structured argument with multiple points. The use of a clear list format and the ability to explain the reasoning behind each tip (e.g., the connection between sleep and energy) shows an independent use of the language. The vocabulary is sufficient to convey the message clearly, despite some awkward phrasing and spelling mistakes."
}
```

```json
{
    "cefr_level": "B1",
    "grammar_score": 0.75,
    "corrections": [
        "I think that we have it... (Remove the comma after 'think')",
        "a to-do list (Avoid all-caps 'TODO')",
        "with a list like this (Remove 'the')",
        "the key tip for a to-do list (Add 'a')",
        "split them into smaller ones (Remove 'the')",
        "If you stick to these tips (Use 'these' for plural)",
        "you wouldn't think (Correct spelling of 'wouldn't')"
    ],
    "feedback": "The student demonstrates a good ability to express complex ideas and provide structured advice. The use of discourse markers ('Firstly', 'Also') and the ability to explain a process (splitting big tasks into smaller ones) are characteristic of the B1 level. While there are several minor grammatical errors—specifically with articles and plural demonstratives ('this tips')—they do not impede communication. The vocabulary is appropriate for the task, and the tone is helpful and natural."
}
```

```json
{
    "cefr_level": "B1",
    "grammar_score": 0.6,
    "corrections": [
        "Everyone got same time a day" -> "Everyone has the same amount of time in a day",
        "study good with it" -> "study well with it",
        "all homeworks" -> "all your homework" (homework is uncountable),
        "Also Its important" -> "Also, it's important",
        "If subject seems not intresting" -> "If a subject seems uninteresting",
        "its not needed in next courses" -> "it's not needed in future courses",
        "get max grade" -> "get the maximum grade"
    ],
    "feedback": "The student demonstrates a B1 level of proficiency. They are able to express complex ideas, such as prioritizing tasks based on their utility for future courses, and use a good range of discourse markers ('To sum up', 'Also'). The vocabulary is appropriate and includes some more advanced terms like 'allocate' and 'priorities'. While there are several grammatical errors (subject-verb agreement, uncountable nouns, and spelling), they do not impede the overall meaning of the text."
}
```

```json
{
    "cefr_level": "B1",
    "grammar_score": 0.7,
    "corrections": [
        "I cannnot agree -> I cannot agree",
        "But there is different situation -> But there is a different situation",
        "in other difficult area -> in other difficult areas",
        "In the university -> At university",
        "And knowledge that university gives is complex -> And the knowledge that a university provides is complex"
    ],
    "feedback": "The student demonstrates a B1 level of proficiency. They are able to organize their thoughts logically into paragraphs and present a clear argument with supporting points. The vocabulary is appropriate for the task (e.g., 'gain work experience', 'confident in themselves', 'complex subjects'), and the text is easy to follow. While there are several grammatical errors—primarily with articles ('a different situation') and noun plurality ('difficult area')—these do not impede the meaning of the text. To reach B2, the student should work on using more varied sentence structures and improving the precision of their grammar."
}
```

```json
{
    "cefr_level": "B1",
    "grammar_score": 0.5,
    "corrections": [
        "Nobody can disagree" -> "No one can deny",
        "has became more polluted place" -> "has become a more polluted place",
        "Does this pollutions affect" -> "Does this pollution affect",
        "making pollution problem looks not vital but dumb" -> "making the pollution problem seem trivial rather than vital",
        "It cause politicians do not look" -> "It causes politicians to ignore",
        "People who cares" -> "People who care",
        "significantly overreacting" -> "are significantly overreacting",
        "climate changes is" -> "climate change is",
        "Trash island" -> "The Great Pacific Garbage Patch",
        "But in more polite way" -> "But in a more polite manner",
        "Collecting all thoughts" -> "In conclusion",
        "only people responsible for pollutions" -> "only people are responsible for pollution",
        "making Earth more clear" -> "making the Earth cleaner"
    ],
    "feedback": "The student demonstrates a B1 level of proficiency. They are able to construct a coherent four-paragraph essay with a clear introduction, body, and conclusion. The student attempts to use complex structures (rhetorical questions, conditional-style reasoning) and a decent range of vocabulary (e.g., 'overreact', 'eco-activists', 'significantly'). However, there are frequent grammatical errors regarding subject-verb agreement, article usage, and word choice (e.g., using 'dumb' and 'crazy' which are too informal for an essay). Despite these errors, the meaning remains clear throughout the text."
}
```

```json
{
    "cefr_level": "B1",
    "grammar_score": 0.7,
    "corrections": [
        "part in becoming -> part of becoming",
        "werelucky -> were lucky",
        "talant -> talent",
        "alwaysbeats -> always beats",
        "On my point of view -> From my point of view",
        "put all the effort into the work -> put all your effort into your work"
    ],

While there are several spelling errors ('talant', 'werelucky') and a few lexical slips ('On my point of view'), these do not impede communication. The text is more advanced than A2 due to the cohesive flow and the ability to express a nuanced opinion, but it lacks the linguistic precision and sophisticated vocabulary required for B2."
}
```
